zh.lifeSummary: 當William Charles Ryder於1510年出生在英國史洛普爾史福斯波利時，他的父親Thomas Ryder30歲，母親Lady Matilda de Umfreville19歲。他於1542年10月2日與Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder在英國英格蘭史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1542年10月2日在英國史洛普爾享年32歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭史洛普爾霍德特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Charles Ryder于1510年出生在英国史洛普爾史福斯波利时，他的父亲Thomas Ryder30岁，母亲Lady Matilda de Umfreville19岁。他于1542年10月2日与Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder在英国英格兰史洛普爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1542年10月2日在英国史洛普爾享年32岁，埋葬在英国英格兰史洛普爾霍德特。

nameMeanings.name: Ryder
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Rider  .
Americanized and Flemish form of Dutch Rijder: occupational name for a mounted warrior or messenger, Middle Dutch rider ‘horseman, rider’.

variants.name: Rider
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English ridere ‘horseman’, perhaps with a specific application such as ‘forest ranger’, ‘horseman who guards crops’, or ‘mounted warrior, knight’. Compare Knight .
English: habitational name from Ryther, near Selby (Yorkshire), probably from Old English ryther ‘clearing’.

English: topographic name from Middle English ridere ‘dweller at a clearing’ (see Reeder ) or ‘dweller by a small stream’, depending on which sense of Middle English ride the surname is derived from. These topographic senses of Rider are limited to the southern counties of England (especially Surrey, Sussex, and Hampshire), where topographic names in -er and the Middle English words ride ‘clearing’ and rithe ‘small stream’ are mainly found. See also Reed .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

en.lifeSummary: When William Charles Ryder was born in 1510, in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, his father, Thomas Ryder, was 30 and his mother, Lady Matilda de Umfreville, was 19. He married Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder on 2 October 1542, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 2 October 1542, in Shropshire, England, at the age of 32, and was buried in Hodnet, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Charles Ryder est né en 1510, à Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Ryder, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Lady Matilda de Umfreville, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder le 2 octobre 1542, à Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 2 octobre 1542, à Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 32 ans, et a été enterré à Hodnet, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Charles Ryder 1510 in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Ryder 30 und seine Mutter Lady Matilda de Umfreville 19. Er hat am 2. Oktober 1542 in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 2. Oktober 1542 im Alter von 32 Jahren in Shropshire, England und wurde in Hodnet, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Charles Ryder è nato il nel 1510 nel Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Thomas Ryder, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Lady Matilda de Umfreville, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder il 2 ottobre 1542, nel Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 2 ottobre 1542, nel Shropshire, England, all'età di 32 anni, e fu sepolto a Hodnet, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: William Charles Ryderが1510年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアシュリュースブリーで生まれた時，父Thomas Ryderは30歳，母Lady Matilda de Umfrevilleは19歳であった。1542年10月2日にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアでLady Margaret Baber Hill Ryderと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1542年10月2日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアで32歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアホードネットにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Charles Ryder(이)가 1510년 영국 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Ryder(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Matilda de Umfreville(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1542년 10월 2일 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1542년 10월 2일 영국 슈럽실르에서 32세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 호드넷에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Charles Ryder nasceu em 1510, em Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Ryder, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Lady Matilda de Umfreville, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder em 2 de outubro de 1542, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 2 de outubro de 1542, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 32 anos, e foi sepultado em Hodnet, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Charles Ryder родился в 1510 г. в местности шрюсбури, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Ryder было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Matilda de Umfreville, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder 2 октября 1542 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 2 октября 1542 г. в местности шропшир, Англия в возрасте 32 лет и был похоронен в местности ходнет, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Charles Ryder nació en 1510, en Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Ryder, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Lady Matilda de Umfreville, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder el 2 de octubre de 1542, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 2 de octubre de 1542, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 32 años, y fue sepultado en Hodnet, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
